0.original: Ideas are fruits of your thinking, but they've got to be harnessed and put to work to have value.
0.b1: Ideas are the result of your thinking, but you must use them to make them valuable.
0.cn: 想法是你思考的果实，但必须加以利用并付诸实践才有价值。

1.original: Each year, an oak tree produces enough acorns to populate a good-sized forest.
1.b1: Every year, an oak tree makes enough acorns to grow a large forest.
1.cn: 每年，一棵橡树产生的橡子足以长成一片大森林。

2.original: Yet, from these bushels of seeds, perhaps only one or two acorns will become a tree.
2.b1: However, from all these seeds, maybe only one or two will become a tree.
2.cn: 然而，在这些成堆的种子中，也许只有一两颗能长成大树。

3.original: The squirrels destroy most of them, and the hard ground beneath the tree doesn't give the few remaining seeds much chance for a start.
3.b1: Squirrels eat most of them, and the hard ground makes it difficult for the rest to grow.
3.cn: 松鼠吃掉了大部分，而树下坚硬的土地让剩下的种子很难生根发芽。

5.cn: 很少有想法能结出果实。

7.original: If we're not on guard, the squirrels, negative-thinking people, will destroy most of them.
7.b1: If we are not careful, negative people will destroy most of our ideas.
7.cn: 如果我们不加防范，那些消极的人就会摧毁我们的大部分想法。

8.original: Ideas require special handling from the time they are born until they're transformed into practical ways for doing things better.
8.b1: Ideas need care from the moment they appear until they become useful tools.
8.cn: 想法从诞生到转化为改进工作的实用方法，都需要特别的呵护。

9.original: Use these three ways to harness and develop your ideas.
9.b1: Use these three ways to manage and grow your ideas.
9.cn: 用这三种方法来掌控和发展你的想法。

11.b1: Do not let your ideas disappear.

13.original: Every day lots of good ideas are born only to die quickly because they aren't nailed to paper.
13.b1: Many good ideas are born every day, but they die quickly because they are not written down.
13.cn: 每天都有许多好想法诞生，但因为没有记在纸上，很快就消失了。

14.original: Memory is a weak slave when it comes to preserving and nurturing brand new ideas.
14.b1: Memory is not good enough to keep and develop new ideas.
14.cn: 在保存和培育新想法方面，记忆力是靠不住的。

15.original: Carry a notebook or some small cards with you.
15.b1: Carry a notebook or small cards with you.
15.cn: 随身携带一个笔记本或一些小卡片。

16.original: When you get an idea, write it down.
16.b1: When you have an idea, write it down immediately.
16.cn: 当你有了想法，就把它写下来。

17.original: A friend who travels a lot keeps a clipboard beside him so that he can write down an idea the instant it occurs to him.
17.b1: A friend of mine keeps a clipboard nearby to write down ideas as soon as he thinks of them.
17.cn: 我的一位朋友随身带着写字板，以便在想法出现的瞬间就能记下来。

18.original: People with fertile creative minds know a good idea may sprout any time, any place.
18.b1: Creative people know that a good idea can happen anywhere at any time.
18.cn: 富有创造力的人知道，好想法随时随地都可能出现。

19.original: Don't let ideas escape, else you destroy the fruits of your thinking.
19.b1: Do not let ideas escape, or you will lose the results of your thinking.
19.cn: 不要让想法溜走，否则你就是摧毁了自己思考的果实。

22.b1: Next, look at your ideas again.

23.original: File these ideas in an active file.
23.b1: Keep your ideas in a special place.
23.cn: 把这些想法归档到一个活跃的文件夹里。

24.original: The file can be an elaborate cabinet, or it can be a desk drawer.
24.b1: This place can be a big cabinet or just a desk drawer.
24.cn: 这个地方可以是一个精致的文件柜，也可以是一个抽屉。

26.original: But build a file, and then examine your storehouse of ideas regularly.
26.b1: Create a file and check your collection of ideas often.
26.cn: 建立一个档案，并定期检查你的想法库。

27.original: As you go over your ideas, some may, for very good reasons, have no value at all.
27.b1: When you review your ideas, you might find some are not useful.
27.cn: 当你回顾这些想法时，有些可能因为各种原因变得毫无价值。

29.original: But so long as the idea has any promise, keep it.
29.b1: But if an idea still seems good, keep it.
29.cn: 但只要这个想法还有希望，就保留它。

32.cn: 现在，让你的想法成长起来。

34.original: Tie the idea to related ideas.
34.b1: Connect the idea to other related ideas.
34.cn: 将该想法与相关想法联系起来。

35.original: Read anything you can find that is in any way akin to your idea.
35.b1: Read anything that is related to your idea.
35.cn: 阅读任何与你的想法相关的内容。

36.b1: Look at the idea from all sides.
36.cn: 从各个角度进行调查研究。

37.original: Then, when the time is ripe, put it to work for yourself, your job, your future.
37.b1: Then, when the time is right, use it to improve your work and your future.
37.cn: 然后，当时机成熟时，将其付诸实践，造福你自己、你的工作和你的未来。
